data.description: represent an attempt to resist a threat that could cause harm, such as a spell, a poison, or a trap. To make a saving throw, roll a d20 and add the appropriate ability modifier. A saving throw can be modified by a situational bonus or penalty, which can result from advantage or disadvantage. Each character or monster has proficiency in at least two saving throws, with class proficiencies determining these. For example, a wizard is proficient in Intelligence saves. Proficiency in a saving throw grants a bonus to the roll made using one's particular ability score. Some monsters have specific saving throw proficiencies.
This section discusses rules related to saving throws in spellcasting and class abilities.
